_meta.dataset: PolicyCentral RBI Enforcement Tracker, seed data
_meta.source: FACE 'A compilation of RBI penalties & enforcement actions in FY 25-26' (Apr 2026)
_meta.note: Illustrative seed spanning every RE type, all reason categories, all scope_fit values, multi-reason and non-monetary actions, plus in-scope exemplars. cited_reasons are plain-language summaries of the RBI-cited reasons (facts only). penalty_amount_display uses the rupee sign.

cited_reasons.text: Extended a bridge loan against amounts receivable from Central/State Government by way of subsidy or reimbursement.
Did not credit (shadow reversal) amounts in unauthorised electronic transactions within 10 working days, and did not compensate certain customers within 90 days of complaint.
Opened/maintained certain current accounts in contravention of regulatory requirements.

records.scope_note: Core-banking and lending-control failures; not a policy-distribution matter.

cited_reasons.text: Allotted CCPS to certain persons whose combined holding exceeded the permitted percentage of paid-up share capital, without ensuring prior RBI approval under Section 12B(1) of the BR Act.
Certain complaints partly/wholly rejected by the internal grievance mechanism were not escalated to the Internal Ombudsman for a final decision.
Did not send final letters to customers on complaint redressal, so customers were not made aware of their right to approach the Banking Ombudsman.
Did not transfer eligible amounts in certain accounts to the Depositor Education and Awareness Fund within the stipulated period.
Did not have face-matching technology in the Video-based Customer Identification Process and did not confirm the customer's economic/financial profile during V-CIP.

records.scope_note: The IO escalation and customer-communication elements relate to SOP dissemination/attestation; the V-CIP face-match is a separate KYC-tech matter.

cited_reasons.text: Levied revised foreclosure charges on certain borrower accounts without incorporating a suitable condition in their loan agreements.

Did not ensure escalation of certain rejected complaints to the Internal Ombudsman within the prescribed time, and did not communicate the final decision to the complainant within the prescribed time in certain cases.

records.scope_note: IO escalation/communication discipline maps to SOP distribution and tracking; the foreclosure-charge clause is a loan-documentation matter.

cited_reasons.text: Did not establish a system of auto-escalation of complaints partly or wholly rejected by the internal grievance redress mechanism to its Internal Ombudsman.

records.scope_note: The automated escalation engine is a complaints-management system; PolicyCentral covers the surrounding SOP dissemination and acknowledgement, not the routing engine itself.

cited_reasons.text: Did not upload the KYC records of certain customers onto the Central KYC Records Registry within the prescribed timeline.
Opened additional BSBD accounts for certain customers who already held BSBD accounts in the bank.

records.scope_note: Timely CKYCR upload is a recurring tracked obligation where a policy/SOP cadence helps; the BSBD duplication is a process-control matter in core systems.

cited_reasons.text: Opened another BSBD account for certain customers who already held a BSBD account in the bank.
Entered into an arrangement with Business Correspondents for activities outside the scope permitted for BCs.
Furnished inaccurate information for certain borrowers to Credit Information Companies.

records.scope_note: Operational-control and data-reporting matters handled in core/credit systems.

cited_reasons.text: Did not report credit information of certain borrowers to the Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (CRILC).

records.scope_note: Regulatory data-reporting pipeline; not a policy-distribution matter.

cited_reasons.text: Routed amounts disbursed and collected in loan accounts through a co-lending escrow account, in violation of the laid-down Fund Transfer Mechanism.
Did not disclose the credit assessment and risk profile of borrowers to prospective lenders, and disbursed loans without the specific approval of individual lenders.

records.scope_note: Platform fund-flow and disclosure mechanics specific to P2P operations.

cited_reasons.text: Did not report Self Help Group member-level data to Credit Information Companies.
Did not identify Beneficial Owners in certain accounts.

records.scope_note: Data-reporting and CDD execution within core/onboarding systems.

cited_reasons.text: The charge of non-disclosure of certain complaints in its annual financial statements for FY 2024-25 was sustained.
End-of-day balance in the escrow account was less than the value of outstanding PPIs and payments due to merchants on certain days, and the shortfall was not reported to RBI immediately.
Failed to obtain prior written permission of RBI for a change in management resulting in change of more than 30 per cent of its directors, excluding independent directors.

records.scope_note: Board/secretarial approval obligation; awareness-driven, not a workforce-distribution matter. High-volume theme for authority content, weak product fit.

cited_reasons.text: Failed to carry out periodic review of risk categorisation of accounts at least once every six months.
Failed to conduct periodic updation of KYC of its customers.

records.scope_note: Both are recurring, calendar-driven obligations where a tracked, attested review policy with reminders is exactly the preventive layer.

cited_reasons.text: Failed to put in place a system of periodical review of compliance with the Fair Practices Code.

records.scope_note: A 'system of periodical review' of a policy's compliance is precisely a tracked, attested, recurring review workflow.

cited_reasons.text: Violated RBI guidelines on outsourcing of financial services by outsourcing core decision-making functions, sourcing, due diligence, disbursement, collections, and KYC verification to a Service Provider in its digital lending operations.

records.scope_note: Business-model/outsourcing-governance breach; included to show the schema handles non-monetary actions.
